trigger: operator 'continue' in same 24h window — subsequent invocation
protocol: §6.N.1.1 subsequent-invocation-in-24h lighter incident-response hunt: 1-2 files most relevant to invocation context
context: Operator's 'continue' followed the F.2 Tasks 1+2+3 commit (c1d6ade6). Hunt scoped to the area of code just touched + the gate-shaping production scanner that backs §6.R IPv4 enforcement.

targets.mutation: Replaced the entire clone-resolution branch in clientFor() with bare `return registry.get(id, MapPluginConfig())` — pre-F.1 behavior that would throw on synthetic clone ids.
targets.expected_failure_signal: java.lang.IllegalArgumentException: Unknown plugin id: rutracker.clone.eu propagates out of the multiSearch coroutineScope.
targets.observed_failure: java.lang.IllegalArgumentException: Unknown plugin id: rutracker.clone.eu — testsuite tests=1 failures=1 errors=0.
targets.rationale: The test claims to guard the pre-F.1 crash regression. The mutation re-introduces that exact crash. The test fires as designed.

targets.purpose: §6.R IPv4-literal enforcement scanner (gate-shaping production code per §6.N.2 canonical list)
targets.mutation: Created tracked .kt file with deliberate `1.2.3.4` literal in non-exempt path (repo-root BluffHuntSample.kt); also included `203.0.113.99` (RFC 5737 doc IP) to verify the doc-IP filter.
targets.expected_failure_signal: scanner exits 1 with `6.R VIOLATION` + path + line number for 1.2.3.4; does NOT flag 203.0.113.99 (filtered out).
targets.observed_failure: exit=1; output: `BluffHuntSample.kt:4:const val REAL_OFFENDER: String = "1.2.3.4"` — exactly the expected behavior. 203.0.113.99 was correctly filtered out.
targets.post_revert_status: scanner exit=0 on the clean tree
targets.rationale: Scanner correctly distinguishes real connection literals from RFC 5737 documentation IPs and from exempt paths.

constitutional_compliance.§6.N.1.1: satisfied — subsequent-invocation-in-24h lighter hunt (1-2 files) performed.
constitutional_compliance.§6.J: satisfied — both mutations targeted user-observable signals (exception propagation, scanner exit code).
constitutional_compliance.§6.L: satisfied — this hunt is the 20th-invocation continuation response.
